prompt_summary: PR #6103 integer dedicated columns — add new configuration section

assertion_results.text: Output includes a 'Files changed' section listing at least one path under docs/sources/tempo/
assertion_results.evidence: Two files changed: docs/sources/tempo/operations/dedicated_columns.md (+51 lines) and docs/sources/tempo/configuration/_index.md (16 changes)

assertion_results.text: Output includes a mapping of PR #6103 to the file(s) updated
assertion_results.evidence: response.md has a PR-to-doc mapping table with two rows for #6103, one per file changed

assertion_results.text: Documented content includes a YAML configuration example
assertion_results.evidence: dedicated_columns.md now includes a full YAML block showing parquet_dedicated_columns with name/type/scope entries for integer attributes

assertion_results.text: Content notes the minimum Tempo version or states 'introduced in Tempo X.Y'
assertion_results.evidence: Diff reviewed — no version callout added to either file. The skill instructions say to 'Call out defaults and version requirements when relevant'. Integer dedicated columns require vParquet5 but no version requirement was noted in the new content.

assertion_results.text: Output includes an 'Open items' section (even if empty)
assertion_results.evidence: Open items section has three substantive items: attribute limit verification, --include-well-known flag guidance, and vParquet5 confirmation

assertion_results.text: Configuration field names in the example match code, not just the PR description
assertion_results.evidence: Agent explicitly corrected type options from 'string' to 'string, int' and scope options from 'resource, span' to 'resource, span, event'. These are code-verified corrections that the PR description alone would not have provided.

notes: High quality output overall. The CLI workflow, output example, YAML block, and configuration corrections are all substantive and accurate. The only failure is the missing version callout (vParquet5 requirement). This is also flagged as an open item (#3), which shows the agent is aware but didn't act on it — a skill instruction gap.
